text: Is there an IC that propagates stably under the Gardner equation v_t + 6 v v_x + (3/2) v^2 v_x + v_xxx = 0 but does NOT remain stable under the full coupled BKdV system when initialized with u_0 = v_0^2/2 (m_0 = 0)? If so, quantify the rate of m drift, the v amplitude decay, the L2 distance to the Gardner solution, and identify which spectral modes amplify first.

hypothesis_or_question: Does v_0 = 1.5 sech^2(x+5) propagate as a clean single peak under the Gardner equation (m=0 reduction of BKdV) over T=10 on x in [-15,15], Nx=256?
design: Run Gardner-only with Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + RK4, dt=2e-4. Track mass_v, L2v (Casimir), Gardner Hamiltonian H = integral(v_x^2/2 - v^3 - (3/8) v^4) dx, v_max(t), v_max_x(t), single-peak count over 21 snapshots in [0, T=10].
method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + explicit RK4, dt = 2e-4, Nx = 256, L = 30

rationale: Gardner with v_0 = 1.5 sech^2(x+5) propagates as a clean single coherent peak over T=10. Mass and L2 norm exactly conserved (<1e-6 ppm); Gardner Hamiltonian conserved to 0.19%; v_max stable to 0.14%; single peak maintained at every snapshot. Empirical phase speed c ~ 3.56. This is the stable positive baseline for the program — the chosen IC is a clean propagating wave in the m=0 reduction.

next_step: E2: rerun the same IC under full BKdV with u_0 = v_0^2/2 using IDENTICAL numerics. Track ||m||_L2(t), v_max(t), u_max(t), and ||v_BKdV(t) - v_Gardner(t)||_L2 against the E1 reference. Test whether the m=0 manifold is dynamically preserved.

hypothesis_or_question: Is the m=0 manifold dynamically preserved by BKdV when initialized with u_0 = v_0^2/2 for v_0 = 1.5 sech^2(x+5)? Quantify ||m||_L2(t), v amplitude, and L2 distance to the Gardner solution.
design: Run full BKdV with u_t + 3 u u_x = -d_x(3 v^2 + v_xx), v_t + 6 v v_x + v_xxx = -d_x(uv). Identical numerics to E1. IC: v_0 = 1.5 sech^2(x+5), u_0 = v_0^2/2 (so m_0 = 0 to machine precision). 21 snapshots matching E1; load round1/snapshots.npz to compute L2 distance per snapshot.
method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + explicit RK4, dt = 2e-4, Nx = 256, L = 30 (same stack as E1)

rationale: BKdV with the Gardner-stable IC + u_0 = v_0^2/2 does NOT preserve the m=0 manifold. ||m||_L2 grows from 0 to 1.22 within t=1 and saturates at 2.55 by T=10. v_max drops from 1.498 to 0.558 (-62.8%), n_peaks goes 1->8, u_max climbs 1.12->3.97. By T=10 the L2 distance to the Gardner solution is 1.81 — larger than ||v_BKdV||_L2 = 0.84 itself, demonstrating that BKdV and Gardner solutions are essentially uncorrelated past t~2 despite identical ICs at t=0. Mass conservation exact (no numerical issue). The Gardner reduction is therefore a kinematic identity, not a dynamically invariant set.

next_step: E3: evaluate the BKdV-S5 algebraic identity m_t|_(m=0) = (v-1)(6 v v_x + v_xxx) on the specific IC v_0 = 1.5 sech^2(x+5). Compute ||source||_L2 (predicted initial growth rate), source spatial support, spectral content (predicted first-amplified k modes), and verify against observed |m_hat(k, t=0.5)|. Also sweep amplitude A to identify the (v-1) sign-flip threshold.

hypothesis_or_question: Does the BKdV-S5 algebraic identity m_t|_(m=0) = (v-1)(6 v v_x + v_xxx), evaluated on v_0 = 1.5 sech^2(x+5), correctly predict (a) the initial growth rate of ||m||_L2 and (b) the spectral content of m at early time?
design: Compute source S(x) := (v_0 - 1)(6 v_0 v_0_x + v_0_xxx) using same spectral stack. Diagnose ||S||_L2, ||S||_Linf, spatial support, top |S_hat(k)| modes. Decompose into S_quad = (v-1)*6 v v_x and S_disp = (v-1)*v_xxx. Load round2 snapshots; compute observed m(x,t) = u - v^2/2 at t=0.5, 1, 1.5, 2; compare ||m||_L2 to t*||S||_L2, compute cosine similarity between observed |m_hat(k, 0.5)|/0.5 and predicted |S_hat(k)|. Sweep A in {0.25, ..., 2.5} and fit log-log slope of ||S||_L2(A).
method: Fourier pseudospectral derivatives (Nx=256, L=30) + post-hoc analysis on round2 snapshots

rationale: The BKdV-S5 algebraic identity is quantitatively predictive. (a) Predicted initial growth rate ||S||_L2 = 1.77/unit-t agrees with observed m_norm(t=0.5)/0.5 = 1.47/unit-t to 17%; ratio drops to ~50% by t=2 as nonlinear saturation engages. (b) The five top-amplifying Fourier modes of m at t=0.5 (n = 4, 3, 5, 2, 6) are the same set as the top five modes of S (n = 4, 5, 3, 6, 2); cosine similarity between |m_hat|(t=0.5)/t and |S_hat| is 0.94. (c) Source decomposes into quadratic-flux piece ||(v-1)*6 v v_x||_L2 = 2.39 and dispersive piece ||(v-1)*v_xxx||_L2 = 1.91 (ratio 1.25). (d) ||S||_L2 vs A has two regimes: slope 0.22 for A in [0.25, 0.75] (dispersive-dominated; v << 1) and slope 2.49 for A in [1.0, 2.5] (quadratic-Burgers dominated; v passes 1 and (v-1) flips sign). At A=1.5 we are above the threshold, source fully turned on, hence rapid breakdown. The mechanism is fully characterized by the algebraic identity.

next_step: Stage-1 program complete. Two bank entries delivered: (POSITIVE) Gardner with v_0 = 1.5 sech^2(x+5) is a stable propagating peak over T=10 with all conservation laws preserved to <0.2%; (NEGATIVE) the same v_0 plus u_0 = v_0^2/2 in BKdV breaks down by t~1, m drifts to 2.55 with cos-sim 0.94 to the predicted source spectrum, L2 distance to Gardner saturates above ||v_BKdV||_L2 itself by T=10. Recommendation for Stage-2: (a) drop the Gardner-soliton-on-m=0 ansatz for BKdV stability work; (b) build a coherent BKdV state numerically before stability analysis; (c) sweep A below the (v-1) sign-flip threshold A=1 to find the slowest-breakdown regime; (d) use the predicted S_hat(k) spectrum on general IC families (Gaussian, bi-soliton, periodic train) to design ICs that minimize off-manifold forcing.
